title: Surrounded Regions
problem_statement: Capture all O regions completely surrounded by X, leaving border-connected O regions unchanged.

examples.explanation: Only the O on the border remains because it reaches the edge.

intuition: Avoid capturing O regions connected to the border by marking them first, then flip remaining O cells.

approaches.name: Border DFS marking
approaches.description: DFS from border O cells to mark safe regions, then convert unmarked O to X while restoring safe cells.
approaches.why_not: Scanning each region individually can mistake border-connected areas; reverse marking is more reliable.

interview_qa.a: Because only O regions not connected to the border should be flipped.
A temporary marker like T distinguishes safe O cells during restoration.
